critic_raw:
Issue 1
Claim: The PR-AUC comparison is valid.
Why wrong: Label maturation mismatch (30-day vs. 90-day) makes this a comparison of different quantities. This is the core flaw.
Evidence: Common-quarter, common-label evaluation required.

Issue 2
Claim: Separate quarters are adequate for evaluation.
Why wrong: Merchant onboarding campaign changes Q2 transaction mix. Population shift confounds model quality.
Evidence: Single-quarter evaluation required.

Issue 3
Claim: A six-basis-point gain at 0.2% prevalence is interpretable without uncertainty bounds.
Why wrong: PR-AUC at rare prevalence is noisy. Bootstrap CI required.
Evidence: Paired bootstrap 95% CI on matched evaluation.
defender_raw: Round 1: Concede all three issues. The current comparison is not valid for the migration decision. Required steps: (a) match labels to 90-day for both models; (b) evaluate on the same quarter; (c) compute paired bootstrap CI.

debate_rounds.critic: Full concession from defender. Critique wins. The corrected evaluation is the empirical test required.
debate_rounds.defender: Round 1: Full concession confirmed. Migration decision should be paused until corrected evaluation is available.

points_resolved: Issue 1: label maturation mismatch — resolved critique_wins
all_issues_raised: Issue 2: different quarters with population shift
Issue 3: no CI for small gain at rare prevalence

empirical_test.condition: Both models evaluated on same quarter with 90-day labels; paired bootstrap 95% CI for PR-AUC difference
empirical_test.supports_critique_if: Gap below 0.003 or CI crosses zero
empirical_test.supports_defense_if: Gap at least 0.006 with CI excluding zero
empirical_test.ambiguous_if: Positive gap below 0.006 with CI barely overlapping zero
